title: Drop panicking is explicitly suppressed in wh_Client_Cleanup
description: client.rs Drop impl logs cleanup failure via eprintln\! but does not panic. The comment says 'panicking in Drop is unsound'. This is correct Rust practice: panicking during drop while another panic is unwinding causes an immediate process abort, which is worse than a logged error. A future contributor might add a panic or unwrap in Drop to 'enforce' cleanup success, but that would introduce abort-on-double-panic in any code path that drops Client during error handling. The eprintln\! approach is the correct tradeoff for a Drop impl on a type that wraps external C resources.
